title: Dietetic Technicians
description: Assist in the provision of food service and nutritional programs, under the supervision of a dietitian. May plan and produce meals based on established guidelines, teach principles of food and nutrition, or counsel individuals.

tasks.text: Observe and monitor patient food intake and body weight, and report changes, progress, and dietary problems to dietician.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct nutritional assessments of individuals, including obtaining and evaluating individuals' dietary histories, to plan nutritional programs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Prepare a major meal, following recipes and determining group food quantities.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise food production or service or assist dietitians or nutritionists in food service supervision or planning.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Plan menus or diets or guide individuals or families in food selection, preparation, or menu planning, based upon nutritional needs and established guidelines.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop job specifications, job descriptions, or work schedules.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Attend interdisciplinary meetings with other health care professionals to discuss patient care.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide dietitians with assistance researching food, nutrition, or food service systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select, schedule, or conduct orientation or in-service education programs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Analyze menus or recipes, standardize recipes, or test new products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Determine food and beverage costs and assist in implementing cost control procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Refer patients to other relevant services to provide continuity of care.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Deliver speeches on diet, nutrition, or health to promote healthy eating habits and illness prevention and treatment.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
